| MCP:zFkRkS |
| MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | # Create React App Firebase Starter Template | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/cra-template-firebase-starter.svg)](https://npmjs.com/package/cra-template-firebase-starter) | MCP:zFkRkS | [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/dy/cra-template-firebase-starter.svg)](https://npmjs.com/package/cra-template-firebase-starter) | MCP:zFkRkS | [![License MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-orange.svg?style=flat)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pettiboy/cra-template-firebase-starter/main/LICENSE) | MCP:zFkRkS | [![PRs Welcome](https://img.shields.io/badge/PRs-Welcome-brightgreen.svg)](https://github.com/pettiboy/cra-template-firebase-starter/pulls) | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ![React](https://img.shields.io/badge/React-20232A?style=for-the-badge&logo=react&logoColor=61DAFB) | MCP:zFkRkS | ![NPM](https://img.shields.io/badge/npm-CB3837?style=for-the-badge&logo=npm&logoColor=white) | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | Make sure you ⭐️ this [`repository`](https://github.com/pettiboy/cra-template-firebase-starter) if you find it helpful or interesting :) | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS |
| MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ## ✨ Installation | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ```bash | MCP:zFkRkS | npx create-react-app your-project-name --template firebase-starter | MCP:zFkRkS | ``` | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | OR | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ```bash | MCP:zFkRkS | yarn create react-app your-project-name --template firebase-starter | MCP:zFkRkS | ``` | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | `NOTE:` This `template` is also available in `TypeScript` [here](https://github.com/pettiboy/cra-template-typescript-firebase). | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ## ⚙️ Usage | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ### SetUp firebase | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | 1. Go to your [`Firebase Console`](https://console.firebase.google.com/) | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | 2. Click on `Add project`. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | - Follow steps and create project. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | 3. Click on web icon. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | - Register web app. | MCP:zFkRkS | - You will get details for setting up `Firebase SDK`. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | 4. Add the config details in your `.env` file (available in root of your project). | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ### SetUp SignIn with google | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | 1. In your project console, click on `Authentication` and `Get Started`. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | 2. In `Sign-in method`, click on `Google`. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | 3. Toggle `Enable` and click on `Save`. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ### To create a new screen | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | 1. Create a new component in `src/screens`. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | 2. In your `src/config/routes.ts`. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ```js | MCP:zFkRkS | { | MCP:zFkRkS | path: "/your-route", | MCP:zFkRkS | component: YourScreenComponentName, | MCP:zFkRkS | name: "Screen Name For Reference", | MCP:zFkRkS | protected: false, // if user needs to be authenticated to access this screen | MCP:zFkRkS | } | MCP:zFkRkS | ``` | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ### To Deploy on GitHub Pages | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | 1. Add your domain (your-github-username.github.io) to the OAuth redirect domains list in the `Firebase console -> Auth section -> Sign in method tab`. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | 2. update the `homepage` field in your `package.json` to the domain you are hosting on. (By default it is: `http://your-github-username.github.io/your-repo-name`) | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | 3. (Optional) Test production build locally: Update the `homepage` field to `/` and run: | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ```bash | MCP:zFkRkS | npm i -g serve # install serve globally via npm | MCP:zFkRkS | npm run build-and-serve | MCP:zFkRkS | ``` | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | 4. Deploy to GitHub Pages run: | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ```bash | MCP:zFkRkS | npm run deploy | MCP:zFkRkS | ``` | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ## ✨ Featues | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ### 1. Routing | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | Routing setup using `react-router-dom`. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | - With easily implementable private routes (routes only authenticated users can use). | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | - Easily add new screens. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ### 2. Authentication | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | Authentication implemented implemented | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | SignIn with Google implemented using `firebase` and `react-firebase-hooks`. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ### 3. Firebase Configured | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | Firebase configured with `GoogleAuthProvider` implemented. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ### 4. Material UI | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | Login Screen UI built using `@mui/material`. | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ### 5. Deploy on Github Pages | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | Deploying on GitHub pages powered by `gh-pages` and [`spa-github-pages`](https://github.com/rafgraph/spa-github-pages) | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ## 🔧 File Structure | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ``` | MCP:zFkRkS | ├── src | MCP:zFkRkS | │ ├── components | MCP:zFkRkS | │ │ ├── auth | MCP:zFkRkS | │ │ | ├── AuthChecker.js | MCP:zFkRkS | │ │ | ├── AuthContainer.js | MCP:zFkRkS | │ │ | ├── Logout.js | MCP:zFkRkS | │ │ ├── utils | MCP:zFkRkS | │ │ | ├── Center.js | MCP:zFkRkS | │ ├── config | MCP:zFkRkS | │ │ ├── firebase.config.js | MCP:zFkRkS | │ │ ├── firebase.js | MCP:zFkRkS | │ │ ├── routes.js | MCP:zFkRkS | │ ├── screens | MCP:zFkRkS | │ │ ├── Login.js | MCP:zFkRkS | │ │ ├── Home.js | MCP:zFkRkS | ``` | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | ## 📝 Authors | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | - Hussain Pettiwala ([@pettiboy](https://github.com/pettiboy)) | MCP:zFkRkS | | MCP:zFkRkS | Special thanks to this well written [`blog`](https://grischuk.de/how-to-create-custom-create-react-app-cra-templates) and this [`repo`](https://github.com/alexandr-g/cra-template-typescript-redux) by [alexandr-g](https://github.com/alexandr-g).
| MCP:zFkRkS | # React app made using firebase-starter template

| MCP:zFkRkS |     },

| MCP:zFkRkS |       "start": "react-scripts start",
| MCP:zFkRkS |       "build": "react-scripts build",
| MCP:zFkRkS |       "test": "react-scripts test",

| MCP:zFkRkS |       "deploy": "gh-pages -d build",
| MCP:zFkRkS |       "pull": "git pull && npm i && npm start"
| MCP:zFkRkS |     },
| MCP:zFkRkS |     "devDependencies": {

| MCP:zFkRkS |     "eslintConfig": {

| MCP:zFkRkS |     }

data.content:
Here's a summary of the npm scripts related to local development and deployment, along with the repository's guidance for testing a production build locally before publishing.

---

## **Local Development & Deployment npm Scripts**

### **Core Scripts (from `package.json`)**
- **`start`**: Starts the local development server (`react-scripts start`)
- **`build`**: Creates an optimized production build (`react-scripts build`)
- **`test`**: Runs the test suite (`react-scripts test`)
- **`eject`**: Ejects the Create React App configuration (irreversible)

### **Deployment Scripts**
- **`predeploy`**: Automatically runs `npm run build` before deployment
- **`deploy`**: Deploys the production build to GitHub Pages using `gh-pages -d build`

### **Template Publishing Scripts**
- **`clean-files`**: Removes the `template/public` and `template/src` directories
- **`copy-files`**: Copies the current `src/` and `public/` into the `template/` directory
- **`prepublishOnly`**: Runs `clean-files` then `copy-files` to prepare the template for npm publishing

---

## **Testing a Production Build Locally (Before Publishing)**

The repository provides clear guidance for testing the production build locally in the generated project (i.e., the app created from this template):

1. **Update the `homepage` field** in your project's `package.json` to `"/"` (to serve the app from the root).

2. **Install `serve` globally** (if not already installed):
   ```bash
   npm i -g serve
   ```

3. **Run the `build-and-serve` script**:
   ```bash
   npm run build-and-serve
   ```
   This script (defined in the template's `template.json`) executes:
   ```json
   "build-and-serve": "npm run build && npm run serve"
   ```
   where `"serve": "serve -s build"` serves the static production build locally.

---

## **Key Notes**
- The `build-and-serve` script is **not present in the template repository's `package.json`** but is included in the generated project via `template.json`.
- For the template repository itself, the `prepublishOnly` script ensures the template's `src/` and `public/` directories are fresh before publishing to npm.
- To deploy to GitHub Pages after testing, simply run `npm run deploy`.
